name: makers-toolkit
description: Discipline for prompting and co-authoring with AI — nothing to configure, just skills. intrinsic-prompt-design: give the model the reason behind a rule, not just the rule; the reasons part clearly helps, even paired with plain imperatives, though so far only anecdotally (rigorous testing is still worth doing), and its bolder idea — deliberately leaving space for the model's own agency to fill — is the core experimental proposition. system-pilot: engineering discipline for deterministic systems, adapting the original Universal CLAUDE.md Protocol. skill-activation-testing: a two-tier A/B (cheap proxy + live firing-counter harness) for whether a description change actually fires, with the validity limit of each tier named.
